evals.prompt: Use goal mode for this Java Spring project: net.example.ErrorCode#PAYMENT_TIMEOUT must propagate to the Controller response. Find all direct and indirect call sites, discuss unclear behavior with me if needed, then create the design and task plan before coding.
evals.expected_output: The agent should confirm independent multi-agent capability, bootstrap records if needed, launch an Investigator Agent before design, identify direct and indirect call chains, ask material clarification questions, create Phase 1 and Phase 2 record documents through the required agents, and pause before implementation.
expectations: The workflow confirms independent multi-agent capability before Phase 1.
The workflow launches or plans to launch an Investigator Agent before writing a design.
The output distinguishes direct call sites, indirect call chains, and boundary callers.
Material uncertainties are surfaced as user-alignment questions before task decomposition.
The workflow creates or names investigation, design, review, and task record paths.
The workflow pauses after Phase 2 before implementation unless the user explicitly requested full execution.

evals.prompt: I want to refactor a large service into smaller services using our CLAUDE.md rules. Please run the multi-agent validation workflow and write task files.
evals.expected_output: The agent should confirm independent multi-agent capability, read project rules, inspect the service and dependencies, produce a requirement/design exploration document, run independent design acceptance, create objective ordered task records, run independent task check, and ask before development.
expectations: The workflow reads project-local rules such as CLAUDE.md before planning.
The workflow uses a Product/Design Agent and a Design Acceptance Agent before task decomposition.
The task plan includes ordered tasks with reasons, measurable goals, and objective acceptance criteria.
The workflow uses an independent Task Check Agent before development.
The workflow records review outputs instead of relying only on chat history.

evals.prompt: First time using this skill in the repo. Set up whatever lightweight templates are needed, but do not install codegraph or other tools unless I approve.
evals.expected_output: The agent should create only local .record directories/templates if missing, require independent multi-agent capability, detect optional capabilities, avoid installing external dependencies, and explain optional enhancements such as codegraph and superpowers.
expectations: The workflow creates only project-local .record directories and templates when missing.
The workflow does not install codegraph, superpowers, or other external tools without explicit approval.
The workflow treats codegraph and superpowers as optional enhancements.
The workflow stops if independent multi-agent capability is unavailable.
The workflow writes or references a goal config with multiAgent.required set to true.
